note: Locations of Lonesome Dove. Coordinates are scholarly estimates — McMurtry kept some geography deliberately loose. approx:true means the novel gives only a region, not a point.

locations.name: Lonesome Dove
locations.blurb: A dusty hamlet on the Rio Grande below Laredo: the Hat Creek Cattle Company and Livery Emporium, the Dry Bean saloon, and not much else. "We don't rent pigs."

locations.name: Pedro Flores's Ranch
locations.blurb: Across the river in Mexico — the Hat Creek outfit's longtime source of involuntary livestock. The herd that goes to Montana starts here, by moonlight.

locations.name: Nueces Crossing
locations.blurb: First river of the drive, and its first grave. Sean O'Brien rides into a nest of water moccasins. Jasper Fant never trusts water again.

locations.name: San Antonio
locations.blurb: Old rangering haunt of Call and Gus, grown citified enough to forget them. Jake squires Lorena through on the way north.

locations.name: The Picnic Grove on the Guadalupe
locations.blurb: The pecan grove where young Gus McCrae courted Clara on picnics, the happiest he ever was. He asks to be buried here — and is, after the longest funeral procession in Western literature.

locations.name: Austin
locations.blurb: Way-station on Jake and Lorena's drift north, where Jake's gambling luck starts to curdle.

locations.name: Fort Worth
locations.blurb: Cowtown where Jake leaves Lorena alone in camp to play cards. The most expensive poker game of his life: Blue Duck is watching the camp.

locations.name: The Storm Plain
locations.blurb: Open country south of the Red where a dry electric storm rolls lightning down the herd's horns and stampedes three thousand cattle. Bill Spettle doesn't come out.

locations.name: Red River Crossing
locations.blurb: Out of Texas and into the Indian Territory. The herd swims it; Texas is behind them for good.

locations.name: Blue Duck's Camp on the Canadian
locations.blurb: In the breaks of the Canadian River, Blue Duck sells Lorena to Ermoke's Kiowas and two buffalo hunters, Monkey John and Dog Face. Gus comes for her at dusk.

locations.name: Where Blue Duck Doubled Back
locations.blurb: The camp where Roscoe, Joe, and Janey waited while the gunmen rode ahead. Blue Duck found them first.

locations.name: Fort Smith, Arkansas
locations.blurb: July Johnson's town: a stray bullet from Jake Spoon's pistol killed Benny Johnson — dentist, mayor, and July's own brother — and set half the book in motion.

locations.name: The Arkansas River
locations.blurb: Elmira's escape route: a flat-bottomed whiskey boat full of buffalo hunters, headed upstream toward a rumor named Dee Boot.

locations.name: The Hanging Ground
locations.blurb: A stand of cottonwoods in western Kansas. Four nooses for the Suggs gang — and a fourth for Jake Spoon, tied by the men he rangered with.

locations.name: Dodge City
locations.blurb: The wicked cowtown everyone talks about and the drive carefully skirts. Jake never gets his hand of cards there.

locations.name: Ogallala, Nebraska
locations.blurb: Last real town before Montana. The boys get their first night on the town; an army scout quirts Newt and learns what Captain Call thinks of rude behavior.

locations.name: Clara's Horse Ranch
locations.blurb: Clara Allen's spread on the North Platte: horses, daughters, a dying husband, and the strongest gravity in the novel. Lorena stays; July stays; Gus almost does.

locations.name: Deets's Grave
locations.blurb: Powder River country, Wyoming Territory. A salvaged plank, carved deep by Call: "Served with me 30 years... Splendid behaviour."

locations.name: Yellowstone Crossing
locations.blurb: The last great river. Po Campo dreads it; the herd swims it anyway. Montana at last — grass to the horizons, and not a fence between here and Canada.

locations.name: Miles City, Montana
locations.blurb: Rough army-and-hide town on the Yellowstone where a doctor takes Gus's left leg and asks for the right. Gus declines. "It's been quite a party, ain't it?"

locations.name: Hat Creek Ranch, Milk River
locations.blurb: Journey's end: sweet grass north of the Missouri, a cabin against the winter, cattle fat by spring. Newt's ranch now, though nobody says the word son.

locations.name: Where Gus Was Hit
locations.blurb: Scouting far ahead of the herd, Gus and Pea Eye are jumped by a hunting party. Gus takes arrows in both legs; Pea Eye slips down the creek by night to bring word.

locations.name: Santa Rosa, New Mexico
locations.blurb: Where the law finally holds Blue Duck for hanging. He leaves through a third-floor window instead, taking a deputy with him, while Call watches from the street.

locations.name: The Sioux Raid
locations.blurb: East of Ogallala on the Platte road. Elmira, fleeing one more time, and Big Zwey, faithfully hauling her, meet a Sioux raiding party.
